general.title: General
general.subtitle: Global application preferences.
general.appearanceHelp: Interface theme. “System” follows your macOS / Windows setting.
general.languageHelp: Interface language. Your choice is kept on this device.
general.sovereigntyBody: Louis sends no data to any third-party Louis server. All requests to AI providers leave directly from your server with your own keys. The FR / EU / US badge shown in the chat indicates where the data exits on each message.
general.aboutBody: Louis · alpha version · AGPL-3.0 · Self-hosted. Your data never leaves your infrastructure except through the AI providers you explicitly configure.

passwordForm.hint: Minimum 10 characters. Using a password manager is recommended.

twoFactor.title: Two-factor authentication
twoFactor.description: Strengthen your account security with a temporary code (TOTP) in addition to your password. Recommended especially for administrator accounts.
twoFactor.enabledDescription: A 6-digit code will be required each time you sign in.
twoFactor.disableLabel: Enter a current 2FA code to disable

enrolling.scanInstruction: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Aegis, 1Password…).

done.title: 2FA enabled
done.backupCodesNotice: Keep these backup codes in a safe place: they let you sign in if you lose your phone. Each one can only be used once. They will never be shown again.

mine.title: My models
mine.subtitle: The models you have added to your platform. Only those listed here appear in the Chat and Board pickers. Browse the library to discover and add new models.

empty.title: No models added.
empty.body: Go explore the library to choose the models you want to make available in Chat and Board.

removed.title: Model removed
removed.description: {label} will no longer be available in the pickers.

toast.orphansPruned: {count, plural, one {# orphan model removed} other {# orphan models removed}}
toast.modelsAdded: {count, plural, one {# model added} other {# models added}}

orphans.title: {count, plural, one {# model without an active provider} other {# models without an active provider}}
orphans.body: They remain in your library but cannot be used until you have a key configured for their original provider.

library.title: Model catalog
library.subtitle: Choose a provider, browse its live catalog, and check the models you want to make available in Louis. Only the models added here will appear in the Chat and Board pickers.
library.providerHint: Choose the key to query its live catalog.
library.idle: Select a provider to see its catalog.
library.returnedSuffix: {count, plural, one {model returned by the {provider} API} other {models returned by the {provider} API}}
library.readyToAdd: {count, plural, one {ready to be added.} other {ready to be added.}}

noProvider.title: Configure a provider first.
noProvider.body: The library queries your providers' API to show you their available models.

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.subtitle: Connect your own Model Context Protocol servers to extend Louis with your in-house or community tools — without modifying the source code.

badge.toolsCached: {count, plural, one {# cached tool} other {# cached tools}}

empty.title: No MCP server
empty.description: Connect a Model Context Protocol server to give Louis additional tools (internal search, intranet documents, CRM database, anything).

explanation.title: What is an MCP server?
explanation.body: The <link>Model Context Protocol</link> is an open standard that lets LLMs call external tools. You can write an MCP server that exposes your internal APIs, your wiki, your CRM, and plug it in here without modifying Louis. Transports supported in v0.1: SSE and streamable HTTP.

addDialog.title: Add an MCP server
addDialog.description: Once added, Louis connects to the server to retrieve its list of tools. Headers (Bearer token, etc.) are encrypted before storage.
addDialog.headersHelp: JSON format. Leave empty if no authentication.

row.deleteConfirmDescription: “{label}” will be deleted. The tools it exposes will no longer be available in conversations. The configuration (URL, secrets) is permanently lost.

memory.title: Case memory
memory.description: Durable facts extracted from your conversations, per case (parties, deadlines, drafting conventions…). A fact only influences responses once approved by you — nothing is used automatically. Each fact stays tied to its case (never shared between clients).
memory.empty: No facts saved yet. Automatic extraction is enabled via LOUIS_MEMORY_EXTRACTION=1.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Mini-instructions that Louis injects into the system prompt when a detector identifies that they are relevant to your request. You create your own skills — nothing is shipped by default, your firm decides.
skills.countLine: {count, plural, one {# skill} other {# skills}} · {active, plural, one {# active} other {# active}}
skills.aside: How it works. On every message, a small model (~1k tokens) looks at your request and the list of active skills. If it detects a relevant one, its system instruction is added to the main model — without any action on your part. Cost: a few thousandths per request.

emptyState.title: No skills yet.
emptyState.descNoTemplates: Create your own skill from scratch.
emptyState.descWithTemplates: Create your own skill from scratch or browse the example library (to adapt to your firm's practice).

formDialog.description: A skill is a mini-instruction that Louis automatically injects into the system prompt when the detector identifies that it is relevant to your request.
formDialog.hintPlaceholder: e.g. When the user cites case law or a legal article
formDialog.hintHelp: Descriptive sentence read by a small model that decides whether or not to activate the skill based on the request.
formDialog.promptPlaceholder: The text that will be injected into the system prompt when the skill is activated…
formDialog.promptHelp: Be explicit, give examples if needed. This instruction takes precedence over the default behavior.

deleteDialog.title: Delete this skill?
deleteDialog.description: “{name}” will be permanently deleted. The AI will no longer activate it automatically.

templates.heading: Example library
templates.description: Seven example legal skills to get started. Import the ones you're interested in — you can then edit or delete them freely.
templates.warning: These examples are provided as starting points. Review them and adapt them to your firm's practice — Louis never imposes a preconfigured prompt.

providers.heading: API key management

moduleHelp.title: Connect an AI provider
moduleHelp.body: Louis runs on a Bring Your Own Key model: add your key, activate it, test the connection. Start with Mistral (🇫🇷) — it powers search across your documents.

providers.intro: Connect your own keys. One rule only: your keys never leave your instance.

security.title: API key security
security.body: All keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being stored in the database. Decryption happens only at the moment of the provider call, server-side. No API call is relayed through a third-party service.

souverains.title: Sovereign
souverains.subtitle: Hosted in France or by the State. Favour these providers for matters covered by professional secrecy.

international.title: International
international.subtitle: The most capable US models. Check your contractual commitments before sending sensitive data.

agregateurs.title: Aggregators
agregateurs.subtitle: A single key to access the multi-provider catalogue (Claude, GPT, Mistral, Llama, Gemini…). Handy as a fallback when a provider is saturated.

selfHosted.title: Self-hosted
selfHosted.subtitle: Self-hosted inference servers (Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp, LiteLLM…) via an OpenAI-compatible API.

dialog.descCreate: Your key is encrypted before being stored (AES-256-GCM). No one can see it, not even the Louis team.
dialog.descEdit: You can change the label or replace the key. Leave a field empty to keep it as is.

delete.title: Delete this key?
delete.description: “{label}” will be deleted. The encrypted key is removed from your instance — conversations using this provider will fail until a new key is entered.

connectors.heading: Legal connectors

moduleHelp.title: Connect your legal sources
moduleHelp.body: PISTE gives access to Légifrance; Pappers to company data. Your credentials stay encrypted on your instance. You can also connect any MCP server.

connectors.intro: Connect your access to French legal sources. Your credentials, your quotas, your contracts — Louis does not get in the way.

security.title: Credential security
security.body: Credentials (client_id, client_secret, API tokens) are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being stored in the database. Authentication with external APIs happens exclusively from your Louis server.

doctrine.description: Case law + enriched doctrine, semantic search.

inpi.description: Trademarks, patents, RNCS — direct access without PISTE.

bodacc.description: Civil and commercial announcements: incorporations, amendments, deregistrations, sales/transfers and insolvency proceedings. DILA open-data source — no configuration required.

dialog.descCreate: Your credentials are encrypted before storage (AES-256-GCM). No call to this API passes through a third-party service.
dialog.descEdit: Edit the label or rotate the credentials. Empty fields are kept unchanged.

delete.title: Delete this connector?
delete.description: “{label}” will be deleted. The encrypted key is removed — the integration will stop working until a new key is entered.

ocr.title: OCR for scanned PDFs
ocr.subtitle: Text-layer PDFs are converted to Markdown 100 % locally. Scanned PDFs (images) require OCR: choose the engine, or let Louis decide automatically.
ocr.degraded: No OCR/vision key configured: Louis uses local Tesseract (sovereign and free, but lower quality on legal text, tables and handwriting). For much better quality, add a Mistral key (dedicated OCR) or a vision provider key (OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic…) in <providers>Providers</providers>.

auto.title: Automatic (recommended)
auto.desc: Mistral OCR if a Mistral key exists → otherwise a vision model via an available key → otherwise local Tesseract.

mistral.title: Mistral OCR (dedicated endpoint)
mistral.desc: Best quality/cost on documents. Requires an active Mistral key.

vision.title: Vision model (your choice)
vision.desc: OCR via a multimodal model of your choice (Pixtral via OpenRouter, GPT-4o, Claude…).

tesseract.title: Local Tesseract (sovereign)
tesseract.desc: 100% local, free, offline. Lower quality on tables and handwriting.

ocr.noMistralKey: No active Mistral key — falling back to Tesseract until one is added.
ocr.noProviderKey: No active provider key. Add one in <providers>Providers</providers> to use a vision model.
ocr.modelHint: The model must accept images. Suggestions for this provider: {suggestions}.

usage.title: Costs & usage.
usage.subtitle: Estimate based on public pricing. Actual values may vary (negotiated discounts, pricing changes).
usage.capReached: Cap reached — your AI requests are blocked until next month or until your firm's administrator raises it.
usage.toolCallsSummary: {count, plural, one {# call} other {# calls}} this month · {rate} % success
usage.noToolCalls: No tool calls this month (Légifrance, Pappers, document search, document generation, MCP).
usage.disclaimer: Costs use providers' public pricing tables (May 2026). For self-hosted models (Ollama, vLLM, Etalab's Albert), the displayed cost is 0 — you pay for the infrastructure elsewhere.
